key_themes: Widespread analyst upgrades and elevated price targets (many $140–$183)
Short-term pressure from sharp crude oil price decline and Middle East geopolitical easing (Hormuz/Iran news)
Strong focus on 2026 cost reductions, production stability, FCF growth, and dividend increases
Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings (April 30) and strategic moves (Venezuela evaluation, potential Permian asset sales)
Viewed as long-term undervalued energy play despite commodity volatility
summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) stock fell sharply (~4.5% on April 17 to ~$116) along with other Big Oil names as crude prices dropped below $90 amid positive Iran-related developments and Hormuz reopening. However, Wall Street sentiment is strongly bullish, with repeated price target hikes (Wells Fargo to $183, Jefferies to $160, others $140–$157) and predominantly Buy/Overweight ratings implying solid upside. With Q1 2026 earnings approaching on April 30, emphasis on cost discipline, LNG/Willow projects, and shareholder returns supports a positive long-term view among investors despite near-term oil volatility.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/COP/)[[2]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/COP)[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/COP/)
sources: 20+ recent news articles and analyst notes (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, CNBC, Zacks, Seeking Alpha), Company announcements (Q4/full-year 2025 results, 2026 guidance, Q1 earnings call on April 30), ~20–25 recent X posts mentioning $COP, analyst targets, and price action
